# TinyCloud JS SDK — Atlas

A machine-readable map of github.com/TinyCloudLabs/js-sdk @ master, intended
for both human readers and agents working in the codebase.

> The repo is the truth. The atlas is the truth, *arranged*.

## What's at /atlas/data/

The atlas serves two audiences from one tree of files:

- **Humans** read it as a website at https://tinycloudlabs.github.io/js-sdk/atlas/
- **Agents** read the same facts as plain JSON, curl-able from /atlas/data/*.json

Every page on the human site is regenerable from these JSON files,
so they cannot drift apart for long.

## Quick endpoints (for agents)

| Endpoint                          | What it is                              |
| --------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| /atlas/data/inventory.json        | Top-level index — packages + chapters   |
| /atlas/data/pkg/<id>.json         | Single package: boundaries, exports, why |
| /atlas/data/flow/<id>.json        | Sequence diagrams (steps array)         |
| /atlas/data/state/<id>.json       | State machines (states + transitions)   |
| /atlas/data/cmds.json             | Command/service surface map             |
| /atlas/data/drift.json            | What changed since last rebuild         |
| /atlas/data/artifacts.json        | All atlas artifact files + freshness    |
| /atlas/data/meta.json             | How this atlas is regenerated (recipe)  |

Package IDs with slashes (e.g. `sdk-rs/web`) are filename-encoded with
`__` (double underscore): `/atlas/data/pkg/sdk-rs__web.json`.

## Quickstart for agents

    curl -s https://tinycloudlabs.github.io/js-sdk/atlas/data/inventory.json | jq .packages
    curl -s https://tinycloudlabs.github.io/js-sdk/atlas/data/pkg/sdk-core.json | jq .exports
    curl -s https://tinycloudlabs.github.io/js-sdk/atlas/data/drift.json | jq '.items | length'

## How to read this in an agent context

1. Start with `inventory.json` — it lists packages, chapters, and where
   to find each one.
2. For "what does package X do?", fetch `pkg/<id>.json` — it carries the
   *why* (one paragraph, hand-curated) plus the structural facts (loc,
   modules, exports, boundaries).
3. For "how does flow Y work?", fetch `flow/<id>.json` — an array of
   steps with from/to/msg.
4. For "what's the command surface?", fetch `cmds.json`.
5. For "is the atlas itself stale?", fetch `drift.json`. If it's
   non-empty, the codebase has moved since the last rebuild.

## Schema

All endpoints are JSON. Top-level shape of inventory.json:

    {
      "$schema": "...",
      "generated": { "at": ISO8601, "by": "tc atlas rebuild", "recipe": "/atlas/data/meta.json" },
      "repo":      { "owner", "name", "ref", "sha", "version", "builtAt", "drift" },
      "packages":  [{ "id", "npm", "kind", "side", "ref": "/atlas/data/pkg/<id>.json" }],
      "chapters":  [{ "id", "title", "voice", "read" }],
      "endpoints": [{ "method", "path", "desc" }]
    }

Per-package shape:

    { "id", "npm", "kind", "side", "modules", "loc",
      "why",       // hand-curated paragraph — the only prose we promise
      "boundaries":[...], "exports":[...],
      "changed", "hasFsm", "hasSeq", "cmds" }

## Regenerating

The atlas is rebuilt by walking `packages/*` for structural facts and
merging them with hand-curated prose in `atlas/curated/*.yaml`. The
recipe is at /atlas/data/meta.json. Run locally:

    bun run atlas:rebuild

After a rebuild, `drift.json` should be empty.

## Source

- Repo:   https://github.com/TinyCloudLabs/js-sdk
- Atlas:  https://github.com/TinyCloudLabs/js-sdk/tree/master/atlas
- Recipe: /atlas/data/meta.json
